body_txt: A Bouncing Battler is, quite simply, a character whose primary form of attack is to jump or bounce off everything in sight — walls, floors, ceilings, and especially opponents. While jumping is a common tactic for acrobatic fighters, the Bouncing Battler is distinguished because he typically doesn't have any weapons or fighting skills ; instead, he relies almost entirely on his mass and momentum to do the work, typically becoming a (figurative) living Hyper-Destructive Bouncing Ball in the process. Fighters like this have a special place in early Platform Games , where Collision Damage may be weaponized by the player or antagonists. For more specific moves, compare Dash Attack and Goomba Stomp . This character is perfectly at home in a Zany Cartoon or a game with permissive Jump Physics . But if the setting is even slightly grounded in realism, only Artistic License – Physics can explain such bouncy mobility. The character's body may be Made of Rubber , justifying its elasticity. They may also be a Master of Motion , able to exert extraordinary control over their kinetic energy and bounce trajectory. Compare Ass Kicks You and Belly Flop Crushing when fat characters use their hefty bodies as bludgeons. Not to be confused with fighting game females with buoyant bodies . Also see Be the Ball , Hyper-Destructive Bouncing Ball , In a Single Bound , Pinball Projectile , and Rolling Attack . Compare Combat Parkour , Trampoline Tummy . Contrast Flash Step , where a character is extremely mobile but generally not bouncy.
